pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word, Google Docs or ODS.
The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you.
You can use either the imperial or metric system to fill in this form. Just
state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

General
● Full legal name: Cassidy
● Age: 31
● Height: 5' 2.5"
● Weight: 124
● Body fat percentage (plus estimation method): 17-20% on a scale,
17% with 3-site calipers
● Years of training experience: 3.5

Strength
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in
weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
● Bench press: 133 lbs x 1
● Squat (excluding body weight): 187 lbs x 1
● Chin-up (excluding body weight): BW x 7
● Overhead press: 68 lbs x 1

What are your fitness goals?
Mostly to get stronger, I've been training mostly powerlifting because
that's what inspires me. But I want to look better too, so I'm willing to
balance that with other focuses. If I get strong enough to have somewhat

No

Will you be performing any other form of exercise alongside
strength training (e.g. soccer, yoga, Olympic Weightlifting)?
No

On the following 7-point scale, how would you rate your
dedication level?
1: I want some results for the minimum amount of effort.
4: I want to strike a reasonable balance between good results in
proportion to effort and sustainability.
7: I will do whatever it takes to achieve maximum results.
5

How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
7

You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

activity level.
Software engineer is a low stress environment with a sit/stand desk

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:
I have a tendency towards tendinitis, developed it badly while running
which is what got me lifting in the first place. One of my legs seems to be
actually shorter than the other slightly (and not just a functional length
discrepancy). That said, I've been doing some pretty high frequency
training (squatting every day for a few months, and now benching and
deadlifting every day) without developing anything. I just need to make
sure my form is spot on and I'm usually ok.

Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?
Pescatarian - basically I eat everything but land animals

Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:

No

BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
This includes when you're at work. If you don't answer this
question acurately with detailed times, you may get a program you
can't follow(!):
I'm only willing to train after work, basically at my current training time
which is about 6:30pm - 8:30pm, the times could be extended some.

Activity level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
● Sedentary (e.g. office job)

Stress level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
● Only occasional/mild stress (e.g. student not during exam period)
headings: Lifestyle

This includes when you’re at work. If you don’t answer this
question acurately with detailed times, you may get a program you

Activity level
Stress level

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
Usually pretty good. I get to bed around 11pm - midnight, wake up
around 6-6:30am. The only problem is if I stay up late on the weekends, I
tend to not be able to sleep in so I often don't get enough sleep then.

How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a
typical work day?
It ramps up. I tend to get to about 600 mg a day and then reset my
tolerance. I recently reset so I'm at about 200 mg a day. Usually coffee in
the morning, and then pre-workout in the evening.

Equipment
Do you have skinfold calipers and if so, which one(s)?
Yes, accu-measure

What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to?
I have fractional plates, .25, .5, .75, 1.

What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of
dumbbells you have access to?
5,8,10,12,15,20,25,30,35

Do you have access to…
● a squat cage or rack? Yes
● a 45° hyperextension bench? No

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

● a reverse hyper machine? No

● a glute-ham raise? No, but I might buy one

● a standing calf raise machine? No
● a dip/chin-up belt? Yes
● a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing? Not a real
one, but I can rig something up with my cables
● a pair of knee wraps? No, but I can buy some
● gymnastic rings? No
● a TRX or similar suspension device? No
● powerlifting bands (not the light home workout stuff)? No, but I can
buy some
● powerlifting chains? No
● a seated calf raise and if so, is it closed chain where you push your
body up (see example image below) or different? No

Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?
I workout at home in my garage.
headings: Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Supplements
Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
Citadel Nutrition Athlete Vitamin

Genetics
● Wrist circumference (smallest point): 6"
● Ankle circumference (smallest point): 8"
● How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was
your height at that point? 5' 2.5" - weight probably around 120 lbs
● Were you heavy as a baby? No
● Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right
hand is clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat
surface with your fingers touching each other and then take a
picture of it.
I did 23andMe, so if there's any results from that you can use, let me
know.

Current Program
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:
IIFYM. It's been pretty sporadic in the last few weeks, but I've been
cutting for a few months (after gaining a lot quickly because of the holiday
season). Been generally doing 5 low days, and 2 higher days. Lower days
about 1150-1250 aiming for 105g protein and letting carbs and fat just
end up how they end up. Higher days have been whatever they end up
being.
headings: Supplements
Genetics
Current Program

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
Weights in pounds. 6 days a week, wednesdays off. 4 day cycle of 1 light
day (135ish deadlift variation 3x10, some bench variation usually incline
DB 3x10ish) 2 moderate days (175-185 conventional deadlifts one day,
185 sumo the second day - 5x3, 100 bench 5x6) 1 heavy day (205 sumo
3x2, 110 3x3 bench).

Randomly amongst that try to hit the following twice a week - shoulders,
squats (only squatting twice a week because I was previously was doing
"bulgarian" squats and improved a lot so now I'm focusing on deads and
bench), hip thrusts and back (including at least pullups or pulldowns, and
some horizontal pull). I do track everything I do in a notebook. But
beyond the deads and bench I'm not following a plan and just decide what
I'm going to do as I'm doing it.
